person_relationships.description: Cabinet colleague; succeeded Wong as Chair of PAP Community Foundation (2022)
Cabinet colleague; both serve in Lawrence Wong's cabinet
Both served as MPs for Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC together (2006-2015)

family_members.name: Teo Eng Cheong
family_members.industry_or_organization: CEO of Sino-Singapore Tianjin Eco-City and Investment Development Co (SSTEC); former CEO of Surbana Jurong

political_stances.stance_content: Said 'you do not need much space to have sex' when asked about HDB flat waiting times for couples wanting families; later acknowledged in 2019 she 'should not have said that'
Commented on Facebook that 'milk is milk, however fancy the marketing', advocating for cheaper infant formula options
Argued against implementing a minimum wage, saying it may lead to higher unemployment and that Singapore's income inequality is 'a problem of success'; advocated Workfare Income Supplement instead

As Manpower Minister during COVID-19, vowed to improve living conditions of foreign workers after severe dormitory outbreaks; implemented three-pronged strategy of containment, lockdowns, and separating essential workers
Introduced and passed the Online Safety (Miscellaneous Amendments) Bill, unanimously passed in Parliament
As Second Minister for Home Affairs, introduced the Online Criminal Harms Act, allowing authorities to take down websites, apps, and online accounts suspected of facilitating criminal behaviour
Introduced Elections (Integrity of Online Advertising) Amendment Bill prohibiting deepfakes of political candidates during election period
Led passage of the Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Bill, establishing a one-stop government Commission for victims of online harms

major_achievements.achievement: Awarded the Rachel Meyer Book Prize for best-performing female candidate in Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences final-year examinations
Appointed CEO of Business China, strengthening Singapore-China economic and cultural ties
Appointed full Minister, becoming one of the few women in Singapore's Cabinet
Managed the COVID-19 foreign worker dormitory crisis as Manpower Minister, implementing quarantine measures and improving living conditions
Appointed Chair of PAP Community Foundation, succeeding Lawrence Wong
Passed the Online Safety (Miscellaneous Amendments) Bill
Introduced the Online Criminal Harms Act
Introduced and passed landmark Elections (Integrity of Online Advertising) Amendment Bill on deepfakes
Led PAP team to win Jalan Besar GRC with 75.21% of vote in GE2025, up from 65.36% in GE2020
Listed by Fortune as one of its 'Most Influential Women' in the Asia-Pacific

biography_summary: Josephine Teo (born Yong Li Min on 8 July 1968) is a Singaporean politician serving as Minister for Digital Development and Information since 2024, Minister-in-charge of the Cyber Security Agency and Smart Nation Initiative since 2021, and Second Minister for Home Affairs from 2017 to 2025. Educated at Dunman High School, Raffles Junior College, NUS (BA and BSS Honours, Rachel Meyer Book Prize) and LSE (MSc Economics, EDB-Glaxo Scholarship), she worked at the EDB (including a posting to the Suzhou Industrial Park), A*STAR, NTUC, and Business China before entering politics in 2006. She held various ministerial roles: MOS/SMOS for Finance and Transport (2011-2017), Minister in PMO (2017), Minister for Manpower (2018-2021) where she handled the COVID-19 dormitory crisis, and Minister for Communications and Information/MDDI (2021-present). She has pioneered multiple online safety and digital governance laws including the Online Safety Act (2022), Online Criminal Harms Act (2023), anti-deepfake elections law (2024), and Online Safety Relief Bill (2025). She is married to Teo Eng Cheong (CEO of SSTEC) with two daughters and a son.

collection_meta.notes: Comprehensive enrichment with detailed legislative record (Online Safety Act, Online Criminal Harms Act, Elections Integrity Bill, Online Safety Relief Bill), COVID-19 dormitory response details, GE2025 results (75.21% in Jalan Besar GRC), Rachel Meyer Book Prize, EDB-Glaxo Scholarship, colleague relationship with Hri Kumar Nair from Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC, LinkedIn account added, predecessor relationship with S. Iswaran. Second Minister for Home Affairs ended 23 May 2025 per Wikipedia.
